creator_bio: Charlotte is a mother of two who runs a successful Amazon FBA retail arbitrage business. She sells a monthly course teaching people how to do exactly what she does. All content is designed to educate her 38,000 followers and funnel them into signing up for her course. She is the leading creator in her niche — very friendly and confident on camera, a true professional at educating her audience. Much more effort goes into her script writing and production than any competitor, which is what sets her apart.
pain_points: Mainly women who are stuck in a 9-5 and want to make money on the side to provide for their family. They want to work from a laptop and manage stock from home around their kids. They've tried side hustles before and failed. They don't know what products to buy or where to find them. They think you need lots of money or experience to start. They're scared of losing money or getting their Amazon account suspended.
transformation: They learn how to find profitable products in UK high street stores, scan them with SellerAMP, and resell on Amazon FBA for real profit — starting with as little as £20. They build consistent income working part time from home around their family. Charlotte's monthly course teaches them everything step by step.
audience_note: Audience is mainly women interested in starting a side hustle. They want to learn retail arbitrage and Amazon FBA. They follow Charlotte because she makes it look achievable — anyone can do this, and her course shows you exactly how.
credibility: Charlotte makes £65,000+ profit in 2 years selling on Amazon part time around her kids. She has sold 5,000+ units and helped over 370 students generate £2.5M in combined sales. She goes in-store every week to find real profitable products live on camera. She uses SellerAMP, Sellerboard, and SellerSnap in her actual business.
phrases_use: Follow for more, in the basket they go, a tidy little sum, let me explain, and here's why, but it only got better, now you know, hit or miss, replenishables, clearance, ungating, profit per item, Q4, restricted, account health
phrases_avoid: Never say 'passive income' or 'get rich quick'. Never use American spellings — use mum not mom, £ not $. No corporate or formal language. Never write long sentences. No hustle culture phrases. Never tell people to click a link — this is almost never used as a CTA. Don't claim unrealistic results without real context.
cta_rules:
IMPORTANT — CTAs follow these rules:
1. Most scripts end with a LOOPING LINE — the last line is designed to connect back into the hook so the video loops seamlessly. The viewer reaches the end and it flows straight back to the beginning, doubling watch time. The loop line should feel like the natural lead-in to the first line of the hook.
2. 'Follow for more' is used occasionally but not on every script.
3. NEVER tell people to click a link or visit bio unless it is specifically a course promo or affiliate script.
4. Comment CTAs ('comment X below') are used for affiliate promos only (SellerAMP, Sellerboard, SellerSnap).
product_placeholder_rule: IMPORTANT — In shopping videos and any script where Charlotte finds a product in store, the product name and exact prices are ALWAYS written as XXXXX because the specific item is unknown until the shoot day. Write XXXXX for product names, £XXXXX for prices that will be filled in on the day. Only use real specific numbers when the script is educational (not a live shopping find).
best_content:
TOP 8 BEST PERFORMING HOOKS (ranked by real performance — gold standard):
1. '10 minutes in Home Bargains' — time challenge
2. 'The best retail park in the UK' — superlative bold claim
3. 'I've stopped selling Pokémon on Amazon, here's why' — controversial admission
4. 'Avoid these 3 stores on Amazon when doing retail arbitrage this week' — warning list
5. 'How to polybag in 20 seconds' — ultra-fast tutorial
6. 'Stop scanning everything, it's killing your time' — calling out bad behaviour
7. 'I just found £20!' — money moment
8. 'Biggest Home Bargains in the UK!' — superlative + store

Hook formats that perform best:
- TIME CHALLENGES: 'X minutes in [store]'
- CALLING OUT: 'Stop doing X', 'Avoid these X', 'Don't buy X'
- CONTROVERSIAL ADMISSION: 'I've stopped doing X, here's why'
- SUPERLATIVES: 'Biggest / Best / Worst / Craziest profit'
- MONEY MOMENTS: 'I just found £X', 'I lost £X'
- Home Bargains is her #1 store for engagement

More top hook examples from scripts:
- 'FREE products in Tesco. And not by working here or stealing them.'
- 'Secret code in Curry's. Which is hidden in plain sight.'
- 'Caught stealing in Tesco. And it's not the first time.'
- 'EMERGENCY!!! There is a current pandemic spreading through Amazon sellers.'
- 'The biggest SCAM of the year is just around the corner.'
- 'I've lost thousands selling on Amazon. But that's allowed me to make £65,000 profit.'

Voice and style:
- Ultra short sentences. One idea per line. Never paragraphs.
- Direct address: you, your, let's, we
- UK stores only: Home Bargains, Boots, B&M, TKMAXX, Tesco, Sainsbury's, Asda, Smyths, Savers, Currys, Dunelm, Ryman, Superdrug, Farmfoods, Lidl, Aldi, B&Q, Waterstones, The Entertainer
- Real failures alongside wins — honest and unpolished
- Affiliate: SellerAMP (scanning), Sellerboard (P&L tracking), SellerSnap (repricer)
looping_example:
Example of a looping script ending:
HOOK: '10 minutes in Home Bargains and I found £140 profit.'
...[body]...
LAST LINE: 'And all of that in just 10 minutes in Home Bargains.'
— The last line echoes the hook so when the video loops, it flows straight back into the opening line naturally.
shop_list: Tesco, Sainsbury's, Morrisons, ALDI, LIDL, TKMAXX, Boots, Currys PC World, Sports Direct, GAME, Argos, B&M, Home Bargains, Smyths, The Entertainer, Waterstones, Farmfoods, The Range, ASDA, Poundland, Savers
topics:
1. Shopping trip / store visit — 'Hit or Miss', live hauls, finding profitable products in UK stores (product names are XXXXX until shoot day)
2. Avoid these brands — account health warnings, restricted or ungated brands to stay away from
3. Store insider secrets — pricing codes, clearance timing tricks, Clubcard/Nectar hacks
4. FBA education — cash flow, ROI, ungating, replenishables, seasonal strategy, Amazon fees explained
5. Profit reveals and credibility — best items this month, student results, Charlotte's own results
product: Charlotte's monthly Amazon FBA course — the primary funnel for all content. Affiliates: SellerAMP, Sellerboard, SellerSnap.
unique_angle: UK female seller and mum of two who goes in-store every week to find real profitable products on camera. Friendly, confident, professional educator. Leading creator in UK Amazon FBA niche with far higher production and script quality than any competitor. Content is specifically designed to funnel women into her monthly course by showing them exactly what is possible.
video_length_note: Scripts range 25–40 seconds. Vary the length — not all scripts should be the same length. Hook always lands in the first 3 seconds.
